title: Lec-31: Hamming Code for Error Detection & Correction both with easiest examples

segments.text: Finally we will talk about Hamming Code for Error Detection & Correction.
Now we will talk about Hamming Code. Before we talked about Hamming Distance,
now we will talk about Hamming Code. So we generally use Hamming Code for
Error Detection & Correction. Now look, remember the first point here,
if Hamming Code is of 7 bits, then 4 bits are for data and 3 bits are for parity.
Approximately you can say we are using 50% because remaining parity bits are redundant,
but again the same thing, to detect and correct you will have to do it.
So similarly if we talk about, look, understand the concept.
Which ones are data bits and which ones are parity.
So I have 7 bits code, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7. I wrote it like this.
Now see which ones are data bits and which ones are parity, for that we use
2 raised to power n formula, where n is 0, 1, 2, 3 and so on.
So look, 2 raised to power 0 is 1, so my first position is parity.
Then 2 raised to power 1 is 2, so second position is parity. You simply have to put,
2 raised to power 2, 2 raised to power 3. So 2 raised to power 2 is 4,
means this is parity. 2 raised to power 3 is 8, 8 is not here,
if it was then that too would be parity. Just remember this point this way.
So remaining is what? Data. So here p0, p1, p2 these three are parity
and d0, d1, d2, d3 are data bits this way. Similarly if I take 11 bits code,
in 11 bits code, I have 4 parity bits
and rest all are data bits. So how do we know which are parity and which are data?
See the position, 2 raised to power 0, 1 is what? Parity bit.
This r1 is parity, r2 is parity, r4 is parity at fourth position,
then parity comes at 8th position, then 16th position, 16 is not here
because it is an 11 bits code. So remaining I have data bits.
Remaining all data bits I have taken. Now what to do? Nothing, now
you simply have to pick the data. Let's say I have code 1010, getting the point?
1010. Means here I have data 1010. I will change the color,
like see this, here I have 1, this is 0, this is 1 and this is 0.
I think it's visible to everyone. This is 1, this is 0, this is 1 and this is 0.
I wrote 1 in d3, 0 in d2, 1 in d1 and 0 in d0, getting the point?
Because this is my MSB, this LSB, so I wrote this according to MSB/LSB.
Now how to calculate p0? How will parity p0 be calculated? Here is the formula.
To calculate p0 you will have to perform XOR between d1, d0 and d3.
How will you know it? You have to calculate p0 or you have to calculate first parity.
So for first parity you have to pick odd number of bits like 1st, 3rd, 5th, 7th and so on.
So when you pick odd number of bits, when you pick d3, d1, d0...
Pick d3, d1 and d0. d0 is here, 0, 1, 1.
011. What is the answer? 011. Now look, 0, 1 and 1, these are what?
These are already even parity. To use even parity or odd, this will also be given to you.
Let's say we are using even parity, or you can say performing XOR operation,
then what is 0 XOR 1? 1. What is 1 XOR 1? 0. So my answer will be 0.
Means in p0 I have 0 bits, getting the point? Similarly I will calculate p1.
To calculate p1, you have to take d0, d2 and d3. How will you know?
p1 means second parity you have to take. So second and third,
then leave the fourth and fifth, take sixth and seventh, leave eighth and ninth.
This way you have to pick 2, leave 2, pick 2, leave 2.
Similarly when you calculate p2, p2 is at which position? Fourth.
So 4th, 5th, 6th, 7th, pick four, then leave four, pick four, leave four.
This way you have to calculate this, so when you calculate parity,
then you put in parity values. Like similarly we calculated p0, then you
calculate p1. How to calculate p1? d3, d2 and d0. d0 is what? 001.
Means if you do between them, then here I will have 1, so my p1 is 1.
Getting the point? Similarly you calculate p2, for p2 what you have to do is this.

like I have shown in the example below. This 11 bit code I have, in this

So we send this code word. Getting the point? So we will send this code word,

then this will detect the error. Because my receiver will also use this same funda.
Receiver will also use this same funda. Similarly it will calculte the parity
and it will know where any problem occurs. Another small example I will show you.
Now I will tell you how to detect. Correction is done, now I will tell you how to detect.
I will show you in this example below, see this. This I have data words,
what's the value in data? 10011011. Getting the point?
Now what do I have to calculate? First r1, means parity1 I calculated,
then I calculated parity2, parity4 and parity8. How did I do it?

When receiver picks it, it will first detect, when it detects it will again
pick these 4 means this code that I gave, it will pick this, calculate the parity,

Now how to correct? What it will do to correct? First it will calculate r1,

1,3,5,7,9,11 when you pick all these bits 1,3,5,7,9,11
then what answer will you get? Wrong answer. Wrong answer means what?
Look, I will do it and show you, 1, 3. Which is 1? This one.
1, 3, 5, 7, 9 and 11. Getting the point?
Now I have intentionally made the ninth bit 1, so that I know that error has occurred.
Now pick all of these, what are the values on them? 1, 1, if you do 1 XOR 1,
then what will be the answer? 0. 0 XOR...
Here if you do 0 XOR 1, then what will be the answer? 1.
If you do 1 XOR 0, then what will be the answer? 1.
If you do 1 XOR 1, then the answer will be 0.
If you do 0 XOR 1, then what will be the answer? 1. Final answer I have is 1.
Getting the point? So when I have the answer as 1, I check parity here.
If we check parity then what answer I got? 1. Let's calculate once again.
11th bit I have is 1,
9th bit I have is 1, I made it 1 intentionally.
7th bit I have is 1,
5th bit I have is 0, 3rd bit I have is 1 and 1st bit I have is 1.
So here calculate the number of 1s, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.
So how many number of 1s are here? 5. What is number of 1s I have? 5.
So if number of 1s is 5, then there is some problem here because this value,

So in this bit this should be 0, then it would be even.
Means this shows me that some error has occurred. So wherever error has occurred,

there was some problem. So you write the problem as 1. Problem means 1 you wrote,
similarly when you calculate r2, do it there will be no problem in it because

there will be no problems in that, parity will be correct because 9th bit did not come in it.
Similarly when you calculate r8 it will have problem because it has 9.
So when problem comes you write it as 1. So when you do r8, r4, r2, r1 this way
then this answer will be what? 9. When you convert it into decimal it will be 9.
From that you will know that in the 9th bit, there is some error.
What is error in the 9th bit? 1. So means this 1 you make 0, because
you can only convert 1 to 0 or 0 to 1. So 1 already I have, receiver sees 1,
Now it calculates that problem is in 9th bit. Then it will convert 1 into 0.
So means here my error is also corrected.
So this way we perform error detection & correction.
So here you have the whole story of how we do error control in Data Link Layer.
